content.full_text:
February 8, 1990

GSX Services of California, Inc. - Legislation - Political Reform Act of 1974

Kathryn Donovan, Esq.
General Counsel
Fair Political Practices Commission
428 J Street, Suite 800
Sacramento, CA 95814

Dear Ms. Donovan:

This letter constitutes a request for informal written advice pursuant to Government Code § 83114(b). In the course of a routine audit, the Franchise Tax Board auditor said that his agency uses a 50% rule for the lobbyist compensation test of 2 Cal.Code Regs. § 18239(b).

Our question concerns an employee of a company who spends less than 50% of his time attempting to influence legislative or administrative action. Is the employee exempt from the definition of lobbyist because he has been engaged "primarily to perform services other than influencing legislative or administrative action" within the meaning section 18239(b)? Would your conclusion be different if the employee spends more than 50% of his time attempting to influence legislative or administrative action?

We appreciate your attention to this matter. If you have any questions, please do not hesitate to contact me.

Very truly yours,

[signature]
Brian W. Maas

cc: Mr. F. K. Lowell
    Mr. E. W. LeBaron, Jr.
    Ms. T. C. Lahaderne
    Mr. A. A. Andersen
